title: 17.-Purchase Order: Operation Pride for tennis programs and tournaments

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a purchase order and any
amendments with Operation Pride to provide youth and adult tennis programs and
tournaments for a total amount not to exceed $150,000 through June 30, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funds for these services are generated directly from Operation Pride’s program and
tournament revenues. The City registers participants, collects the revenue, and provides
facilities, then pays Operation Pride from the revenue collected to cover their services. The
amount of actual expenditures will vary from year to year depending on the number of
participants enrolled in the program. Funding is available in the FY25 and FY26 Camps
Fund budget, and additional amounts will be requested through the Annual Appropriations
process as needed.
sections.current_situation:
Operation Pride runs twenty to twenty-five USTA-sanctioned tennis tournaments annually
in addition to year-round tennis programs at City facilities, serving thousands of participants
per year. The City registers program participants and collects fees through its registration
software platform, and then pays Operation Pride a percentage of the tournament revenues
for their services on a regular basis. The number of participants has grown each year for
these fee classes/programs and additional purchasing authority is needed to continue this
service for a growing number of Berkeley residents.
sections.background:
The City has partnered with Operation Pride for over two decades to offer tennis programs
and tournaments to Berkeley residents. Community members learn tennis skills and
compete in tournaments at a variety of skill levels.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Operation Pride provides a sustainable model for the afterschool program and tournaments
by re-using tennis balls and tournament materials. Additionally, Operation Pride is based

E-mail: manager@CityofBerkeley.info Website : http://www.CityofBerkeley.info/Manager

locally which reduces the miles driven and vehicle carbon emissions generated by program
participants.
sections.rationale:
The City of Berkeley Parks Recreation and Waterfront Department staff does not have the
skill or capacity to offer high quality tennis programming. Contracting for these services with
Operation Pride provides a high quality service that meets community needs.
sections.alternatives:
None
CONTACT PERSON
Scott Ferris, Director, Parks, Recreation & Waterfront Department, 981-6700
Stephanie Chu, Recreation and Youth Services Manager, 981-6700
Justin Pitcher, Recreation Program Supervisor, 981-5123
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
PURCHASE ORDER: OPERATION PRIDE TENNIS PROGRAMS AND TOURNAMENTS
WHEREAS, Operation Pride runs twenty to twenty-five USTA-sanctioned tennis
tournaments annually in addition to year-round tennis programs at City facilities, serving
thousands of participants; and
WHEREAS, the City registers program participants and collects fees through its registration
software platform, and then pays Operation Pride a percentage of the tournament revenues
for its services on a regular basis; and
WHEREAS, the number of participants has grown each year for these fee classes/
programs and additional purchasing authority is needed to continue this service for a
growing number of Berkeley residents.
WHEREAS, FY25 and FY26 funds are available in the Playground Camp Fund (fund 125)
to cover this contract, and additional amounts will be requested during the annual
appropriations process as needed.
NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the City
Manager is authorized to execute a purchase order and any amendments with Operation
Pride to provide youth and adult tennis programs and tournaments for a total amount not to
exceed $150,000 through June 30, 2026. A record signature copy of said contract and any
amendments to be on file in the Office of the City Clerk.
